# Iglesia ni Cristo Knowledge Base

## About Iglesia Ni Cristo

<!-- @section: about_inc, type: general -->
The *Iglesia Ni Cristo* (Church Of Christ) is a Christian religion with the primary purpose of worshiping Almighty God in the manner taught by the Lord Jesus Christ and His apostles as recorded in the Bible. The church is for all people, regardless of race, nationality, or social status. As of recent data, it comprises 152 racial and ethnic backgrounds, with nearly 7,000 congregations in 168 countries and territories.

---

### Mission and Vision
<!-- @section: mission_vision, type: general -->
**Vision: A Radiant Church**
Based on Ephesians 5:27, the Church is envisioned as a "radiant church, without stain or wrinkle or any other blemish, but holy and blameless." It aims for perfect unity in faith and practice, with every member devoted to a life of holiness and spiritual maturity.

**Mission: A Church that Shares**
Based on Mark 16:15-16 and Colossians 1:28, the Church's mission is to continuously preach the gospel of salvation from the Bible. This involves propagating the Church and edifying the faithful, guiding them in proper worship and a righteous way of life leading to salvation.

---

### History
<!-- @section: history, type: general -->
- The *Iglesia Ni Cristo* was preached by Brother Felix Y. Manalo, considered God’s Messenger in these last days, and was officially registered in the Philippines on July 27, 1914.
- The first congregation was in Punta, Sta. Ana, Manila, and it quickly spread throughout the Philippines.
- By the time of Brother Felix Y. Manalo's passing on April 12, 1963, the Church had districts in over half of the Philippine provinces.
- In 1968, under the leadership of Brother Eraño G. Manalo, the Church began its international expansion, establishing its first overseas congregations in Honolulu, Hawaii, and San Francisco, California.
- Expansion continued into Europe (1972), Australia (mid-70s), Asia (1974), Africa (1978), and Latin America (1990).
- Congregations were established in key historical Christian cities: Rome, Italy (1994); Jerusalem, Israel (1996); and Athens, Greece (1997).
- Under its current Executive Minister, Brother Eduardo V. Manalo, the Church continues to grow globally, constructing and renovating houses of worship worldwide.
- On July 21, 2014, the Church inaugurated the Philippine Arena, the world's "largest mixed-use indoor theatre," for its centennial celebration.

---

## Core Beliefs

### The Holy Scriptures
<!-- @section: holy_scriptures, type: beliefs -->
The Bible is the sole basis of faith and service to God. It is believed to be inspired by God and profitable for teaching, correction, and instruction in righteousness (II Tim. 3:15-17). The Church holds that God’s words are hidden in mystery and can only be correctly understood and taught by messengers sent by God (Mark 4:11-12; Rom. 10:15). Preaching by those not sent by God is considered "another gospel" that leads to perdition (Gal. 1:6-7; II Pet. 3:15-16).

---

### One God, the Father
<!-- @section: one_god, type: beliefs -->
The one and only true God is the Father, the Creator (John 17:3, 1; I Cor. 8:6). God is a spirit (John 4:24) with no flesh and bones (Luke 24:39). He is from everlasting to everlasting, immortal, and the sole Creator of all things (Ps. 90:2; Neh. 9:6).
<!-- @exclusion: trinity, exception: "none" -->
The Church does not believe in the trinity of persons in God. The Bible points to the Father alone as the true God.

---

### The Lord Jesus Christ
<!-- @section: jesus_christ, type: beliefs -->
The Lord Jesus Christ is the Son of God, the one and only Savior, and the sole mediator between God and man (Matt. 3:17; I Tim. 2:5).
<!-- @exclusion: jesus_is_god, exception: "none" -->
The Church does not believe that Christ is a God-Man. He is man in nature, as testified by Himself and His apostles (John 8:40; I Tim. 2:5). He experienced human attributes like hunger, thirst, and weariness, and ultimately died (Matt. 4:2; John 19:28). However, He is distinct from all other men as He did not sin (I Pet. 2:21-22). God has exalted Him and given Him a name above all names (Phil. 2:9-11).

---

### The Holy Spirit
<!-- @section: holy_spirit, type: beliefs -->
The Holy Spirit is the power sent by the Father in Christ's name to teach, remind, and help God's messengers and the Church (Acts 1:8; John 14:26). It strengthens, edifies, and comforts the Church.
<!-- @exclusion: holy_spirit_is_god, exception: "none" -->
The Holy Spirit is not "God the Holy Spirit." It is a spirit sent by God but is not God Himself.

---

### God's Messengers
<!-- @section: messengers_of_god, type: beliefs -->
The task of understanding and teaching God's words from the Bible is given exclusively to the messengers of God (Rom. 10:15). Brother Felix Y. Manalo is believed to be God's messenger in these last days, fulfilling prophecies from Revelation 7:2-3 and Isaiah 43:5-6. God reveals the meaning of His words to His messengers through the Holy Spirit (II Pet. 1:20-21).

---

### God's Election
<!-- @section: gods_election, type: beliefs -->
Since ancient times, God has set apart His people to serve Him (Ps. 4:3). After the fall of Israel, God's election was transferred to the Church Of Christ (I Pet. 2:9). The first-century Church apostatized, and in these last days, God sent a messenger from the Far East (Philippines) to preach the pure Gospel and re-establish the true Church (Rev. 7:2-3).

---

### The Church and Salvation
<!-- @section: church_and_salvation, type: beliefs -->
Membership in the Church Of Christ is believed to be the only way for people to receive salvation. All people have sinned and need a savior (Rom. 5:12). To be saved, one must enter into Christ's body, which is the Church (John 10:9; Col. 1:18), because it is the Church that Christ will save (Eph. 5:23). Christ will not save anyone outside His Church, as that would be against God's law that each person must pay for their own sin (Deut. 24:16).

---

## Practices and Duties

### Worship of God
<!-- @service: worship_services, emergency: false -->
It is the whole duty of man to worship God and keep His commandments (Ps. 95:6-7). Worship must be done in spirit and in truth (John 4:23-24). The *Iglesia Ni Cristo* observes worship services which include hymn-singing, prayers, studying God's words, and voluntary offerings (I Cor. 14:26). Congregational worship service is of extreme importance and members are strictly forbidden to forsake it (Heb. 10:25-27). Church services are held twice a week in houses of worship.

### Baptism
<!-- @service: baptism, emergency: false -->
The Church believes baptism commanded by Christ is done by immersion (Rom. 6:4; John 3:23). To qualify, a person must be taught God's words, believe in them, repent, and renew their life (Mark 16:15-16; Acts 2:38).
<!-- @exclusion: infant_baptism, exception: "none" -->
Infant baptism is not practiced because an infant cannot fulfill the prerequisites for baptism. Infants are instead dedicated to God (Matt. 19:13-15).

---

### Christian Unity and Brotherhood
<!-- @section: unity_and_brotherhood, type: general -->
The Church subscribes to absolute unity, where God, Christ, and the Church are joined together (John 17:23, 21). Division is strictly forbidden (I Cor. 1:10). Members uphold the love of brotherhood, addressing one another as brother and sister (I Pet. 2:17). Hating a brother or sister is considered a grave sin (I John 3:15). Members with disputes are commanded to reconcile (Matt. 5:23-25).

---

### The Church Administration
<!-- @section: church_administration, type: structure -->
Members are duty-bound to submit to the Church Administration as a commandment from God (Heb. 13:17). The Administration is entrusted with the ministry of reconciliation (II Cor. 5:18-20). To abide by the Administration's decisions is to abide by the words of Christ (Acts 16:4; Luke 10:16). The leadership structure includes the Executive Minister, Brother Eduardo V. Manalo, and the Deputy Executive Minister, Brother Angelo Eraño V. Manalo.

---

## Eschatology (End Times Beliefs)

### Judgment Day
<!-- @section: judgment_day, type: beliefs -->
The Church believes in the Lord Jesus Christ’s promise that He will return (John 14:1-3). The exact day and hour are unknown to all except God the Father (Matt. 24:36). Signs heralding His return include wars, famines, and earthquakes (Matt. 24:6-8), which began fulfillment in 1914 with World War I. This day will be the Day of Judgment and destruction of the ungodly, when the heavens and earth will be destroyed by fire (II Pet. 3:7, 10).

---

### The Resurrection of the Dead
<!-- @section: resurrection, type: beliefs -->
The Church believes the dead will be resurrected. There are two kinds of resurrection: one to life for those who have done good, and one to condemnation for those who have done evil (John 5:28-29). Deceased Church Of Christ members will be resurrected on Christ's return, and the faithful who are alive will meet Christ in the air without dying (I Thess. 4:16-17).

---

### The Holy City, New Jerusalem
<!-- @section: holy_city, type: beliefs -->
The Holy City is a glorious place prepared by Christ for His faithful disciples (John 14:2-3; Rev. 21:10-21). Life there is perfect, with no need for sun or moon, no shortages, no curse, no sorrow, and no death (Rev. 22:1-5). Only those given by the Father to Christ, who are called into the Church Of Christ, can enter the New Jerusalem (John 17:12; I Cor. 1:9).

---

## Membership

### How to Become a Member
<!-- @service: bible_studies, emergency: false -->
The process to become a member of the Iglesia Ni Cristo is established by Christ and involves several steps:
1.  **Learn the Teachings:** A person must learn the basic Bible teachings upheld by the Church through a series of Bible study lessons.
2.  **Probation Period:** Undergo a probationary period to prove genuine belief through obedience to the Bible's teachings. This period is a minimum of six months.
3.  **Baptism:** Be baptized by immersion into the Church Of Christ.
<!-- @faq: how_to_become_member -->
*   **Q: How do I become a member?**
    *   **A:** The process involves attending Bible study lessons to learn the fundamental teachings, undergoing a probation period of at least six months to demonstrate sincere belief, and finally being baptized by immersion into the Church.

---

## Activities and Outreach

### Edifying the Members
<!-- @section: member_edification, type: community -->
The Church provides programs to strengthen the faith and relationships of its members through Christian Family Organizations (CFOs):
-   ***Buklod***: For married members, focusing on strengthening husband-and-wife relationships.
-   ***KADIWA*** (Youth with Noble Intent): For single members 18 years and older, preparing them for the future and married life.
-   ***Binhi*** (Seedling): For baptized members aged 12 to 17, instilling the importance of education and right living.
-   ***Pagsamba ng Kabataan (PNK)*** or Children’s Worship Service (CWS): For members' children aged 4 to 11, teaching God's words through age-appropriate methods.
-   **Other Groups:** Christian Brotherhood International (CBI) for students, Christian Employees Association (CEA), Christian Medical Dental and Paramedical Society (CMDPS), Christian Society for the Deaf (CSD), and Society of Communicators and Networkers International (SCAN).

---

### Evangelism & Media
<!-- @section: media_platforms, type: media -->
The Church uses mass media to spread its message globally:
-   ***Pasugo: God’s Message***: The official monthly magazine of the Church.
-   ***INCRadio (DZEM 954kH)***: An AM radio station that began broadcasting in 1969.
-   ***INCTV***: The flagship television station of the Church, featuring all-INC programming.
-   **Websites and Social Media:** Official sites include `iglesianicristo.net`, `incmedia.org`, and `pasugo.com.ph`, along with various social media pages.
-   **Streaming Apps:** The INC Media mobile app is available on iOS and Android, and content is streamable on Roku, FireTV, and AppleTV.

---

### Socio-Civic and Community Programs
<!-- @service: care_for_humanity, emergency: false -->
The Church is concerned with the welfare of society and undertakes various socio-civic projects:
-   **Care for Humanity (*Lingap sa Mamamayan*)**: An outreach program providing immediate assistance to calamity victims, the indigent, and others in need, implemented by the Felix Y. Manalo (FYM) Foundation.
-   **New Era General Hospital**: Caters to the health concerns of members and mobilizes volunteers for medical missions.
-   **New Era University**: Offers educational programs from basic to post-graduate levels.
-   **Community/Housing Projects**: Establishes resettlement communities with housing, livelihood sites, and worship buildings for those in need.
-   **INC Giving Project**: Encourages volunteers to engage in acts of kindness in their communities, such as clean-up drives, appreciation days for community helpers, and donations to shelters.

---

### Events and Competitions
<!-- @section: church_events, type: community -->
The Church holds various events to nurture brotherhood and showcase members' talents:
-   **INC Unity Games & EVM Cup**: Multi-sport events promoting physical fitness and Christian unity.
-   **INCinema**: A film and stage play competition promoting Christian values, culminating in the Excellence in Visual Media (EVM) Awards.
-   **Musical & Artistic Competitions**: Includes INConcert, Oratorio, International Piano Competition, INC Voice Competition, and Songs of Faith, Love, and Hope.
-   **Other Contests**: EVM Art (painting/sculpture), Essay and Poetry Writing.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

<!-- @faq: response_time -->
*   **Q: When is Jesus Christ coming back?**
    *   **A:** The Bible states that no one knows the day and hour of Christ's return except God the Father (Matthew 24:36). However, there are signs of the end times, such as wars, famines, and earthquakes, which began to be fulfilled in 1914.

<!-- @faq: jesus_is_god -->
*   **Q: Is Jesus God?**
    *   **A:** No. According to the Bible, Jesus is the Son of God, not God Himself. He is a man in nature (John 8:40), though distinct from all others as he was without sin. The one and only true God is the Father.

<!-- @faq: wrong_trinity -->
*   **Q: Why is the trinity wrong?**
    *   **A:** The doctrine of the Trinity is not found in the Bible. The Scriptures consistently point to the Father alone as the one true God (John 17:1, 3).

<!-- @faq: who_is_felix_manalo -->
*   **Q: Who is Felix Y. Manalo? Why do you believe he is God's last messenger?**
    *   **A:** Felix Y. Manalo is believed to be God’s messenger in these last days, prophesied in the Bible (e.g., Revelation 7:2-3). His authority is testified by the fulfillment of prophecies concerning his place of origin (the Far East), the nature of his work, and the time period of his commission.

<!-- @faq: why_one_true_church -->
*   **Q: Why do you say the Church Of Christ is the one true Church? Won’t all churches be saved?**
    *   **A:** Not all churches will be saved. The Bible teaches that the true Church is the one Christ established, which He purchased with His blood (Acts 20:28 Lamsa). To be saved, one must be a member of this one body or Church.

<!-- @faq: how_to_be_saved -->
*   **Q: How can I be saved?**
    *   **A:** To be saved, one must enter into the body of Christ, which is the Church Of Christ (Eph. 5:23). This is achieved by learning and believing in God's words, repenting, renewing one's life, and being baptized into the Church.

<!-- @faq: why_did_jesus_die -->
*   **Q: Why did Jesus have to die?**
    *   **A:** The wage for sin is death (Romans 6:23). Because all have sinned, all face eternal punishment. Jesus Christ, who was without sin, shed His blood to purchase the Church (Acts 20:28 Lamsa), thereby redeeming its members. By creating "one new man" with the Church as His body and Himself as the head, His sacrifice could answer for the sins of the body without violating God's law that each must pay for their own sin.

<!-- @faq: bible_translations -->
*   **Q: Why are there so many Bible translations, and which one does the INC use?**
    *   **A:** The INC references various translations of the Bible to clearly and faithfully convey God's message in a given language. Different versions can help clarify the original meaning of the text.

<!-- @faq: who_can_preach -->
*   **Q: How can we tell if God is speaking to us through a pastor? Can anyone preach the gospel?**
    *   **A:** No, not everyone has the authority to preach. A true preacher sent by God must speak according to the law (God's doctrines written in the Bible) and the testimony (the biblical prophecies that validate their authority). They will not teach their own opinions but will read and explain the teachings directly from the Scriptures.

<!-- @faq: eat_blood_prohibited -->
*   **Q: Why is eating blood prohibited?**
    *   **A:** The Bible prohibits the eating of blood because God designated blood to give life and to redeem man from sin, not for consumption.

<!-- @faq: sabbath_observance -->
*   **Q: Why don’t you observe the Sabbath?**
    *   **A:** The Sabbath was a command for a specific people (ancient Israel) during a specific time. The Church Of Christ follows the biblical teachings applicable to the Christian era.

<!-- @exclusion: holidays -->
*   **Q: Why don't you celebrate Christmas, Easter, or other holidays?**
    *   **A:** The Church does not observe religious holidays that have pagan roots or are not commanded in the Bible, as the Scriptures prohibit adding to or subtracting from God's words.

<!-- @faq: interfaith_marriage -->
*   **Q: Why are INC members not allowed to date or marry someone outside the Church?**
    *   **A:** The Church follows the biblical teaching to not unite in marriage with non-believers, as it is believed this can lead members away from God and their faith.

---

## Directory and Locations

### Global Presence
<!-- @coverage: include -->
The Iglesia Ni Cristo has a global presence with congregations in 168 countries and territories, organized into ecclesiastical districts.

**Main and Regional Offices:**
-   **Central Office (Philippines):** No. 1 Central Ave., New Era, Quezon City 1107, Philippines.
-   **North America Office:** 770 Airport Boulevard, Burlingame, CA 94010, USA.
-   **Europe Office:** 79 Staines Road, West Sunbury-on-Thames TW16 7AH, United Kingdom.
-   **Africa Office:** 88 Alexandra Road, King William's Town 5601, South Africa.
-   **Australia Office:** #3 Ross St., North Parramatta, NSW 2151, Australia.
-   **Northeast Asia Office:** 2 Chome 14-2, Kita-Magome Otaku, Tokyo 143-0021, Japan.

A full directory of local congregations and their worship service schedules can be found at `directory.iglesianicristo.net`.

---

## Legal & Privacy

### Terms of Use
<!-- @section: terms_of_use, type: legal -->
Users of the Iglesia Ni Cristo websites (`iglesianicristo.net`, `incmedia.org`) must accept the Terms of Use. Key restrictions include prohibitions on reproducing, commercializing, or damaging website material. The content is the intellectual property of CEBSI/INC. Disputes are governed by the laws of the Philippines.

---

### Privacy Policy
<!-- @section: privacy_policy, type: legal -->
The privacy policy outlines the collection, use, and disclosure of personal data. Data is collected for service provision and improvement. This may include personal details, cookies, and usage data. Data may be shared with third-party service providers (e.g., web hosting, analytics). Users have rights regarding their data, including access, deletion, and rectification. All data is controlled and processed by CEBSI in the Philippines. By using the services, users consent to this policy.



The following list details the coherent topics and subtopics found within the **combined_INC.md** document, providing summaries and verbatim excerpts from the source material.

# 1. Global Identity and Mission
**Summary:** The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) is an international Christian religion dedicated to worshiping God according to the teachings of Jesus Christ, serving a diverse membership across six continents.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) is a Christian religion whose primary purpose is to worship the Almighty God in a manner taught by the Lord Jesus Christ and His apostles as recorded in the Bible. The Church Of Christ is a church for all peoples. It is for everyone who will embrace the true faith regardless of race, nationality, cultural background, social standing, economic status, and educational attainment."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Thus far, the Iglesia Ni Cristo has an international membership comprising 152 racial and ethnic backgrounds. It maintains nearly 7,000 congregations and missions grouped into more than 178 ecclesiastical districts in 168 countries and territories in the six inhabited continents of the world."

# 2. Fundamental Doctrines and Core Beliefs
## 2.1 One God, the Father
**Summary:** The Church asserts that the Father alone is the true God and Creator, and it explicitly rejects the non-biblical concept of the Trinity.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "We believe that the one and only true God is the Father, the Creator. We hold this belief because this is the doctrine taught by our Lord Jesus Christ and His Apostles (John 17:3, 1; I Cor. 8:6). God is a spirit (John 4:24), and, therefore, He has no flesh and bones (Luke 24:39)."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "There is no trinity of persons in God. Though the Bible speaks of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit, never does it refer to all of them as gods nor as three persons in one God; rather, it points to the Father alone as the true God. The Son Himself emphasized that only the Father is the true God (John 17:3, 1) and that He Christ Himself is a man telling the truth which He received from God (John 8:40)."

## 2.2 The Lord Jesus Christ
**Summary:** While recognized as the Son of God and Savior, Jesus Christ is believed to be man in nature rather than God.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "The Iglesia Ni Cristo believes that the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of God (Matt. 3:17), is the one and only Savior given by God (Acts 13:23). Christ is the sole mediator between God and man (I Tim. 2:5) and the only way to God (John 14:6). But we do not subscribe to the belief that Christ is a God-Man or both God and man. He is man in nature according to His own testimony (John 8:40) and the teachings of His Apostles (I Tim. 2:5; Matt. 1:18)."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "The attributes of a human being are found in Christ. He hungered (Matt. 4:2), thirsted (John 19:28), became weary or tired (John 4:6), slept (Matt. 8:24), and died (Matt. 27:50; I Cor. 15:3). However, Christ as distinct from all other men, is the only one Who did not sin (I Pet. 2:21-22; Heb. 4:15)."

## 2.3 The Holy Spirit and Holy Scriptures
**Summary:** The Holy Spirit is defined as the power of God, not a deity, and the Bible is upheld as the exclusive basis of faith.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "We believe that the Holy Spirit is the power (Acts 1:8) sent by the Father in the name of Christ, to teach and remind His messengers of all things that Christ said (John 14:26). ... and although the Holy Spirit is a spirit, never does the Scriptures refer to it as “God the Holy Spirit” because it is not God but one of the spirits being sent by God."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "We believe that the Bible is the sole basis of faith and service to God. The Gospel is the power of God unto salvation to those who believe, for in it the righteousness of God is revealed (Rom. 1:16-17)."

# 3. The Church, Salvation, and Membership
**Summary:** Membership in the Iglesia Ni Cristo is taught as the only means to receive salvation because Christ is the Savior of His body, the Church. The joining process requires 28 Bible lessons and a six-month probation period.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "The Iglesia Ni Cristo believes that membership in the Church Of Christ is the only means for people to receive the salvation that the Savior Jesus Christ brings. ... mankind are to die in the lake of fire, the wages of sins (Rom. 5:12; 6:23; Rev. 20:14). For people to be saved, he should enter in by Christ, by becoming a member of His body or Church (John 10:9; I Cor. 12:27; Col. 1:18) because it is His Church that Christ will save (Eph. 5:23)."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "The shortest amount of time it can take to be baptized inside the Church Of Christ is 6 months. The process includes listening to the 28 Bible study lessons regarding the Basic Bible Teachings and consistently for 6 months."

# 4. Prophecy, the Last Messenger, and Administration
**Summary:** Brother Felix Y. Manalo is identified as God's Messenger in these last days, fulfilling biblical prophecy. Members are duty-bound to follow the Church Administration.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "We believe that the late Brother Felix Y. Manalo is God’s messenger in these last days; he was the fulfillment of the Biblical prophecies concerning the messenger from the Far East (Rev. 7:2-3) who would preach to God’s sons and daughters in our time (Isa. 43:5-6; 46:11; 41:9-11)."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "The Iglesia Ni Cristo believes that every member is duty-bound to submit himself to the Church Administration because it is God’s commandment (Heb. 13:17). This is so because God entrusted to the Administration the message and the ministry of reconciliation (II Cor. 5:18-20). To submit to the Administration is to be bound by its instructions (Matt. 18:18). To abide by the decisions laid down by the Administration is to abide by the words of Christ (Acts 16:4; Luke 10:16)."

# 5. Worship Services and Practices
**Summary:** Worship services are twice-weekly solemn gatherings featuring hymns, prayers, and biblical study. The Church rejects tithing, emphasizing voluntary offerings.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "A solemn gathering of the faithful, the worship service of the Iglesia Ni Cristo is usually held twice a week in the house of worship and consists of hymn-singing, prayers, voluntary offering, and the study of God’s words to guide the true Christians in their daily living."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "We don’t practice tithing. ... First of all, who should give? What did He say? Each one should give. How should our offering be given? The Bible says, “as he purposes in his heart.” So not 10% anymore as it was during the Old Testament times. Now, it’s what the person purposes in his heart."

# 6. Christian Family Organizations (CFO)
**Summary:** The Church maintains several organizations to edify members at different life stages, including Buklod for married couples, KADIWA for single adults, and Binhi for youth.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Buklod (“bond”), the organization for married members envisions a strong husband-and-wife relationship leading to a family that is steadfast in faith and active in serving God. ... KADIWA or Kabataang may Diwang Wagas (Youth with Noble Intent), the organization for single members 18 years old and above, aims to prepare them for a bright future as well as for a married life under God’s blessing."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Binhi (seedling), the organization for baptized members 12 to 17 years old, instills in the youth the importance of knowledge and education while guiding them in right living and proper conduct. ... Pagsamba ng Kabataan (PNK) or Children’s Worship Service (CWS) caters to the members’ children, 4 to 11 years old."

# 7. Signs of the Times and Judgment Day
**Summary:** Biblical prophecies regarding wars, famines, and earthquakes, particularly since 1914, indicate that Judgment Day is near.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Jesus now tells His followers how they could discern when the world was actually entering into the beginning of the birth pangs … The first such time in world history occurred during the years of World War I (1914−1918) …"
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "The day of Christ’s return is the Day of Judgment and destruction of ungodly men. On that day, the heavens will pass away with a loud noise, and the elements will be dissolved with fire and be burned up (II Pet. 3:7, 10)."

# 8. Socio-Civic Outreach and Humanitarian Aid
**Summary:** Through projects like "Care for Humanity" and the Felix Y. Manalo Foundation, the Church provides disaster relief and assistance to those in need globally.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "The Care for Humanity (Lingap sa Mamamayan) outreach program has provided immediate assistance to multitudes of people, from calamity victims to indigents and indigenous peoples, and all others who are in need. Through its implementing arm, the Felix Y. Manalo Foundation, the Church seeks to give aid to the needy in all parts of the world."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Across the United States on Saturday, May 10, in 26 different locations, the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ), in partnership with the Felix Y. Manalo Foundation, held simultaneous Care for Humanity events to benefit community members and to commemorate the 139th birth anniversary of God’s Messenger in these last days, Brother Felix Y. Manalo."

# 9. Christian Living and Relationships
## 9.1 Parent-Child Relationship
**Summary:** Biblical values guide family life, emphasizing the parents' duty to teach God's commands and the children's duty to respect and obey parents.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Parents are responsible for raising their children in a particular way, not just any way that they choose. Since God is the one who blesses the couple with children, He knows what’s best for them, which is to make sure that the children grow up in His discipline and teachings."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Children, it is your Christian duty to obey your parents, for this is the right thing to do. “Respect your father and mother” is the first commandment that has a promise added: “so that all may go well with you, and you may live a long time in the land.”"

# 10. Contact Information and Directory
**Summary:** The Church maintains administrative offices and congregations worldwide to serve its international membership.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) Central Office No. 1 Central Ave., New Era, Quezon City 1107 Philippines Tel. No. (632) 8981-4311, Fax No. (632) 8981-4333."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "NORTH AMERICA 770 Airport Boulevard, Burlingame CA 94010 USA Fax: (650) 548-1128."

# 11. Media Resources and Evangelism
**Summary:** The Church uses flagship media like INCTV and INCRadio, along with various websites, to propagate the gospel.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Pasugo: God’s Message, the monthly official magazine of the Church, carries mainly religious articles and Church news and features. ... Its official website is at pasugo.com.ph. Aside from the magazine, the Church also has other publications such as books, pamphlets, leaflets, and fliers for worldwide distribution."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "INCTVis the flagship television station of the Church Of Christ. The Church first ventured in TV evangelism with the “Ang Iglesia Ni Cristo” program which premiered over a major TV network in the Philippines in February 1983."

# 12. Legal Policies and Terms of Use
**Summary:** Accessing Church-operated websites constitutes a binding agreement regarding content restrictions, liability, and privacy policies.

*   **Verbatim Detail:** "By using this website, you (the ‘user’) are taken to have understood and accepted the Terms of Use and Privacy Policy below, and have agreed to be bound by it. ... You are prohibited from: Reproducing and publishing any Website material in other media... Selling, sublicensing and/or otherwise commercializing any Website material."
*   **Verbatim Detail:** "Christian Era Broadcasting Service International (‘CEBSI’) is the data controller and processor for all information collected as part of your use of the website (the ‘Service’)."



KB_IGLESIA_NI_CRISTO_COMPREHENSIVE.MD
Purpose: This document is the authoritative knowledge base for the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ)
. It is structured for long-context memory processing using the bchat annotation system
. For behavioral rules, see POLICY.MD. For conversation flow, see SCRIPT.MD.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. Global Identity and Mission
The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) is a Christian religion whose primary purpose is to worship Almighty God in the manner taught by the Lord Jesus Christ and His apostles as recorded in the Bible
.
• International Scope: Comprised of members from 152 racial and ethnic backgrounds
.
• Global Presence: Maintains nearly 7,000 congregations grouped into more than 178 ecclesiastical districts in 168 countries and territories across six inhabited continents
.
• Leadership: Led by the Executive Minister, Brother Eduardo V. Manalo
.
• Vision: A "radiant church, without stain or wrinkle or any other blemish, but holy and blameless" (Ephesians 5:27)
.
• Mission: To continuously preach the gospel of salvation and edify the faithful toward spiritual maturity
.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

3. Core Beliefs and Doctrines
• The Only True God: We believe the one and only true God is the Father, the Creator
. There is no trinity of persons in God; the Father alone is the true God (John 17:3, 1)
.
• The Nature of Jesus Christ: Jesus is the Son of God and the sole mediator between God and man (1 Tim 2:5)
. He is man in nature, not a "God-Man". He hungered, thirsted, slept, and died
.
• The Holy Spirit: The power (Acts 1:8) sent by the Father in the name of Christ to teach and strengthen the Church
. It is not "God the Holy Spirit"
.
• The Holy Scriptures: The Bible is the sole basis of faith and service to God
.
• The Church and Salvation: Membership in the Church Of Christ is the only means to receive salvation because Christ is the Savior of His body, which is the Church (Eph 5:23)
.
• The Last Messenger: Brother Felix Y. Manalo is God’s Messenger in these last days, fulfilling prophecies regarding the "messenger from the Far East" (Rev 7:2-3; Isa 43:5-6)
.
• Church Administration: Members are duty-bound to submit to the Church Administration as it is God's commandment (Heb 13:17) to manage the ministry of reconciliation
.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. Prohibitions and Non-Observances
• Pagan Holidays: The Church does not celebrate Christmas, Easter, Halloween, or Valentine's Day due to their pagan roots
.
• Dietary Laws: The Bible strictly prohibits the consumption of blood

• Relationships: Members are not permitted to date or marry non-believers (un-believers)

• Marriage: The Church prohibits divorce, adhering to the sanctity of marriage

• Religious Artifacts: Rejects the use of the cross as a symbol of Christianity

• Infant Baptism: Not practiced; candidates must be old enough to be taught, believe, and repent


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5 Services and Practices
• Worship Services: Solemn gatherings held twice a week consisting of hymn-singing, prayers, voluntary offerings, and the study of God's words

• Bible Studies: Conducted in a strict question-and-answer format where answers come directly from the Bible without personal opinion

• Holy Array: Attendees wear clean, conservative clothing (no jeans/sneakers; skirts or dresses for women)

• Offerings: Tithing (10%) is not practiced Offerings are voluntary, prepared beforehand, and given as one "purposes in his heart" (2 Cor 9:7)

• Baptism: Performed by immersion for those who have completed the required lessons and probation


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6 Membership and Frequently Asked Questions
• How to Join: The 3-step process includes: 1) Learning the 28 Bible lessons, 2) Completing a 6-month probation period of consistent attendance, and 3) Baptism by immersion

• Minors: Individuals under 18 require parental approval to sign up as a Bible student

• Judgment Day: No one knows the day or hour
 However, "signs of the times" (World War I, famines, earthquakes) indicate the return of Christ is near

• The "Far East": The place where the Church would emerge in the last days, fulfilled by the Philippines


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7 Organizations and Outreach
• Christian Family Organizations (CFO):
    ◦ Buklod: For married members

    ◦ KADIWA: For single members 18 and older

    ◦ Binhi: For members aged 12 to 17

    ◦ PNK/CWS: Children’s Worship Service for ages 11 and below

• Specialty Groups: SCAN (Emergency/Radio), CMDPS (Medical), CBI (Students), CSD (Deaf)

• Humanitarian Aid: Conducted through the Felix Y Manalo (FYM) Foundation and the Care for Humanity (Lingap sa Mamamayan) program


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8 Emergency and Spiritual Distress
• Distress Protocol: For those feeling lonely or weak, seek God’s mercy (Psalm 25:16-17)

• Refuge: The house of worship is the designated place to seek help and prayer during hardships

• Pastoral Care: Seekers are encouraged to schedule a free video "Meet & Greet" with a Minister to address urgent spiritual questions


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9 Official Media Resources
• Websites: incmediaorg, iglesianicristonet, pasugocomph

• Television: INCTV (Flagship station) and NET25

• Radio: INCRadio DZEM 954kH

• Apps: INC Media App (iOS, Android, Roku, FireTV, AppleTV)

• Directory: iglesianicristonet/directory


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10 Global Ecclesiastical Districts (Examples)

• Asia: Hong Kong, Macau, Malaysia, South Korea, Taiwan, Thailand, Tokyo, etc

• Europe: Britain, Central Europe, Ireland, Italy, Mediterranean, Spain

• Africa: Northern, Southeastern, and Southern Africa

• Australia-Oceania: New South Wales, New Zealand, Queensland, Victoria


## Global Identity and Mission
The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) is an international religious organization established to worship God according to biblical teachings.

### About the Iglesia Ni Cristo
The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) is a Christian religion whose primary purpose is to worship the Almighty God in a manner taught by the Lord Jesus Christ and His apostles as recorded in the Bible. It is a Church for all peoples, welcoming everyone who embraces the true faith regardless of race, nationality, cultural background, social standing, economic status, or educational attainment.

Currently, the Church has an international membership comprising 152 racial and ethnic backgrounds. it maintains nearly 7,000 congregations and missions grouped into more than 178 ecclesiastical districts in 168 countries and territories across the six inhabited continents of the world. The Church is envisioned as a "radiant church, without stain or wrinkle or any other blemish, but holy and blameless," as described in Ephesians 5:27. This vision drives a perfect unity of faith and practice, where every member is devoted to a life of holiness founded on true Christian teachings.

The mission of the Church is to continuously preach the gospel of salvation written in the Bible. By propgating the Church and edifying the faithful, the organization guides its members in the proper worship of God and a righteous way of life. This global expansion began in earnest in 1968, when the first local congregations outside the Philippines were established in Honolulu, Hawaii, and San Francisco, California.

### History and Reestablishment
The Iglesia Ni Cristo was preached by Brother Felix Y. Manalo and was registered in the Philippines on July 27, 1914. From its first local congregation in Punta, Santa Ana, Manila, the Church quickly spread throughout the Philippines. By 1963, ecclesiastical districts had been established in more than half of the provinces in the country. Following the leadership of Brother Eraño G. Manalo, the Church expanded into the Far West and eventually around the entire world.

The Church believes its reestablishment in the last days is supported by biblical prophecy. It identifies Brother Felix Y. Manalo as God’s messenger in these last days, fulfilling prophecies regarding a "messenger from the Far East" who would preach to God’s sons and daughters in the time period referred to as the "ends of the earth." The Church continues to progress under the leadership of the present Executive Minister, Brother Eduardo V. Manalo, with constant increases in membership and the construction of magnificent houses of worship worldwide.

## Fundamental Doctrines
The bedrock of the Church's faith is rooted in the exclusive authority of the Holy Scriptures.

### The Only True God
We believe that the one and only true God is the Father, the Creator. This belief is based on the doctrines taught by Jesus Christ and His Apostles. God is a spirit and does not have flesh and bones. There is no trinity of persons in God; while the Bible speaks of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit, it never refers to them as three persons in one God. Instead, it points to the Father alone as the true God.

The Son himself emphasized that only the Father is the true God and that he, Christ, is a man telling the truth which he received from God. The prophets also taught that there is only one Father who created us. God is from everlasting to everlasting, immortal, and does not tire or become weary. He alone is the Creator of the heavens, the earth, and all things within them.

### The Lord Jesus Christ
The Iglesia Ni Cristo believes that the Lord Jesus Christ is the Son of God and the one and only Savior given by God. He acts as the sole mediator between God and man and is the only way to God. However, the Church does not subscribe to the belief that Christ is a "God-Man." He is man in nature according to his own testimony and the teachings of the Apostles.

The attributes of a human being are found in Christ: he hungered, thirsted, became weary, slept, and died. Distinct from all other men, however, Christ is the only one who did not sin. He has been exalted by God and given a name above all other names. Eventually, Christ will subject all his power and authority to the true God. He never appropriated the title "God" or "God the Son" for himself because he is not God, but a man.

### The Holy Spirit and Holy Scriptures
The Holy Spirit is the power sent by the Father in the name of Christ to teach and remind his messengers of all things that Christ said. The Holy Spirit strengthens, edifies, and comforts the Church. Although the Holy Spirit is a spirit, the Scriptures never refer to it as "God the Holy Spirit" because it is not a deity but a power sent by God.

The Bible is held as the sole basis of faith and service to God. The Gospel is the power of God unto salvation for those who believe. The Holy Scriptures are inspired by God and profitable for teaching, reproof, correction, and instruction in righteousness. God has purposely hidden His words in mystery so that those striving to understand through worldly knowledge cannot know the truth. Only messengers sent by God have the authority to interpret these mysteries accurately.

## Membership and Salvation
The Church teaches that membership is essential for the protection of the soul and the attainment of eternal life.

### The Necessity of the Church
The Iglesia Ni Cristo believes that membership in the Church Of Christ is the only means for people to receive salvation. Because all have sinned, mankind is destined to die in the lake of fire, which is the wages of sin. To be saved, one must enter into Christ by becoming a member of his body, which is the Church. Christ is the Savior of his body, and he will not save anyone outside of it, as that would violate God's law requiring sinners to pay for their own sins.

The Church is the "one new man" created by Christ, where he is the head and the members are the body. By being part of this body, the members are redeemed by the blood of Christ. This redemption allows them to be forgiven of their sins and reconciled with God, changing their status from being separated from God to being his children.

### The Path to Membership
Becoming a member involves a specific process established to ensure the seeker understands and believes the fundamental doctrines. The steps include:
*   **Learning the Teachings:** Prospective members must listen to 28 Bible lessons regarding basic teachings.
*   **Probation Period:** Seekers undergo a minimum six-month probation period to prove their faith through consistent attendance at worship services and obedience to God's commands.
*   **Baptism:** Membership is finalized through baptism by immersion, as commanded by Christ and practiced by the Apostles.

Individuals under the age of 18 require parental approval before signing up as a Bible student. The Church does not practice infant baptism because a person must first be taught the words of God, believe, and repent before being baptized.

## Church Practices and Services
Worship and daily conduct are guided by the instructions found in the Bible.

### Worship Services
Solemn gatherings of the faithful are held twice a week in the house of worship. These services consist of hymn-singing, prayers, voluntary offerings, and the study of God’s words. These lessons are intended to guide Christians in their daily living. The Church emphasizes the extreme importance of these services, and members are strictly forbidden from willfully forsaking them.

Attendees are expected to wear "Holy Array," which consists of clean, conservative clothing fitting for a holy occasion. This generally means no jeans or sneakers; women wear skirts or dresses. The worship is conducted in spirit and in truth, with the goal of giving glory to God.

### Offerings and Tithing
The Iglesia Ni Cristo does not practice tithing (giving 10% of one's income). Instead, offerings and contributions are performed as a commandment of God that should be voluntary and not compulsory. Every member gives according to the dictates of their heart, as "God loves a cheerful giver."

Contributions are prepared beforehand and given in proportion to the blessings and gain that God bestows on the individual. These offerings are used for the expenses of the Church, including the construction and maintenance of houses of worship and humanitarian projects.

### Prohibitions and Non-Observances
Members of the Church adhere to several biblical prohibitions and do not observe holidays with pagan roots.
*   **Holidays:** The Church does not celebrate Christmas, Easter, Halloween, or Valentine's Day.
*   **Dietary Laws:** The Bible strictly prohibits the consumption of blood.
*   **Marriage:** Members are not allowed to marry non-believers, as this can lead them away from God.
*   **Divorce:** The Church does not allow divorce, adhering to the biblical teaching on the sanctity of marriage.
*   **Religious Artifacts:** The use of the cross as a religious symbol is rejected.

## Organizations and Outreach
The Church provides various platforms for spiritual growth and community assistance.

### Christian Family Organizations (CFO)
The CFOs aim to strengthen the brotherhood and promote a vibrant spiritual life within every household. They include:
*   **Buklod:** For married members, focusing on strong husband-and-wife relationships.
*   **KADIWA:** For single members aged 18 and above, preparing them for a bright future.
*   **Binhi:** For youth aged 12 to 17, focusing on right living and education.
*   **CWS (Children’s Worship Service):** For children aged 4 to 11, using age-appropriate methods for biblical instruction.

Specialty groups also exist, such as **SCAN** for emergency response and licensed radio communication, and the **Christian Medical Dental and Paramedical Society (CMDPS)** for medical missions.

### Socio-Civic Outreach
The Church is concerned with the welfare of society and undertakes various humanitarian programs. The **Care for Humanity** (Lingap sa Mamamayan) program provides immediate assistance to calamity victims, indigents, and indigenous peoples. These efforts are often implemented through the **Felix Y. Manalo Foundation**.

In addition to relief goods, the Church has developed resettlement communities like Barrio Maligaya for victims of religious persecution and Sitio New Era for victims of Typhoon Haiyan. The Church also operates the **New Era General Hospital** and **New Era University** in the Philippines.

## Frequently Asked Questions
Common inquiries regarding beliefs, practices, and the end of the world.

### Is Judgment Day near?
While no one, not even the Lord Jesus Christ, knows the exact day or hour, the Bible provides "signs of the times" that indicate the end is near. These signs include wars, famines, earthquakes, and increasing wickedness. The Church identifies the outbreak of World War I in 1914 as the beginning of these "birth pangs." Judgment Day will be a day of fire and the destruction of ungodly men.

### Why is the Trinity rejected?
The word "Trinity" and the concept of three persons in one God are not found in the Bible. The Church follows the teaching that there is only one true God, the Father. Jesus Christ is the Son of God and a mediator, but he is distinct from the Father and is man in nature.

### How can I find a congregation?
The Church maintains a global directory of its congregations. Seekers are encouraged to visit the official website at **iglesianicristo.net/directory** to find the nearest house of worship and check service times.

## Official Media Resources
Official channels used to propagate the faith and provide spiritual content.

### Media Outlets
The Church utilizes mass media to reach a broader audience with the message of the Bible.
*   **Pasugo: God’s Message:** The official monthly magazine containing religious articles and Church news.
*   **INCTV:** The flagship television station featuring an all-Iglesia Ni Cristo program format.
*   **INCRadio (DZEM 954kH):** An AM radio station that also features live-streaming and a mobile app.
*   **Websites:** Major resources include **incmedia.org**, **iglesianicristo.net**, and **pasugo.com.ph**.
*   **INC Media App:** Available on iOS, Android, Roku, and Apple TV for on-demand Christian programming.
